name: Upgrade Shop
description: A shop-like progression interface where players spend currency or materials to improve stats, abilities, equipment, or account perks.
design_notes: Upgrade shops combine economy transactions with permanent progression. They need upgrade_id, prerequisite requirements, cost curves, purchase limits, stat recalculation, and rollback rules so buying an upgrade feels reliable and cannot desync wallet and progression state.
design_purpose: Give players a clear place to convert resources into durable power or utility.
Pace upgrades through costs, tiers, prerequisites, and stock or availability rules.
Create a predictable alternative to random reward acquisition.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are investing earned resources into a stronger build or account.

parameters.name: upgrade_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the purchasable upgrade.

parameters.name: upgrade_cost_curve_id
parameters.description: Formula or table used to calculate the current upgrade cost.

parameters.name: prerequisite_upgrade_ids
parameters.description: Upgrades or milestones required before this upgrade can be purchased.

parameters.name: purchase_limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of times this upgrade can be bought within its scope.

parameters.name: upgrade_effect_payload_id
parameters.description: Data payload applied after the purchase transaction commits.

edge_cases: A player can afford an upgrade when the panel opens but spends the resource elsewhere before confirmation.
An upgrade changes a stat that affects current health, stamina, inventory capacity, or active cooldowns.
Prerequisite upgrades are refunded or removed after dependent upgrades have already been purchased.
A content patch changes upgrade_cost_curve_id and must migrate already purchased tiers safely.
common_bugs: Wallet is debited but upgrade_effect_payload_id fails to apply, leaving the save in a half-purchased state.
Purchase buttons check prerequisites in UI but the commit path accepts invalid prerequisite_upgrade_ids.
Stackable upgrades exceed purchase_limit after save/load because count is stored in UI state.
Derived stats update twice when both the purchase event and save reload apply the same effect.
balancing_notes: Upgrade costs should be tuned against actual currency and material source rates, not only desired tier order.
Permanent upgrades need escalating costs, prerequisites, or caps to avoid flattening challenge too early.
Offer parallel upgrade paths so the shop supports build choice rather than a single mandatory shopping list.
accessibility_notes: Show current level, next effect, full cost, missing resources, and purchase limit in one readable view.
Provide confirmation or undo for expensive upgrades that permanently change a build.

implementation_notes.unity: Commit upgrades through a transaction service that validates upgrade_id, prerequisites, purchase_limit, wallet/material costs, applies upgrade_effect_payload_id, recalculates stats, and saves once.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep upgrade catalog data separate from shop UI nodes; purchase_upgrade should run through a progression service and emit committed state changes for UI refresh.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use DataAssets/DataTables for upgrade definitions and an authoritative component or subsystem for purchase validation; replicate committed upgrade state and derived stat changes.
implementation_notes.web: Model upgrade purchase as a single reducer action with before/after resource and progression diffs; persist purchased upgrade IDs and counts rather than derived UI state.

relationships.reason: Upgrade shops spend wallet currency or materials to grant persistent stat or ability improvements.
Upgrade costs can absorb recurring idle income so passive earnings do not inflate without sinks.
Upgrade shops can grant abilities through purchase rather than level or story gates.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses upgrade interfaces to convert resources into persistent options.

example_games.title: Rogue Legacy
example_games.note: Uses estate upgrades as long-term progression purchases.
